queue.title: Video sync: pause videos when not visible — IDLE pauses fgVideo+bgVideo; ACTIVE pauses genericVideo; tab hidden pauses all
queue.note: setDialState(IDLE) must pause fgVideo + bgVideo. setDialState(ACTIVE) must pause genericVideo. onVis (tab hidden) must pause all active videos and resume on return. Pool videos (poolPrev/poolNext) are exempt — they play off-screen to stay buffered. Also pause outgoing visibleVideo when demoted to pool slot after a swap.

queue.title: Video sync: dedicated RAF drift monitor — pause ahead video if |fg - bg| > threshold; only runs in ACTIVE/ENGAGED
queue.note: Replace syncFgToBg() in draw() with a separate RAF loop scoped to dialState !== IDLE. Threshold: 0.1s desktop / 0.3s mobile. Use intentionallyPaused flag to avoid fighting tab-hidden or deliberate pauses. Cancel in cleanup/destroy.

queue.title: BG video sliding-window pool: bgPoolPrev/bgPoolNext in applyActive + setDialState uses bgVideoRef
queue.note: ROLLED BACK: 2026.2.27.6 added a 3-rAF re-sync in playPaired to close bg decode startup lag (~50ms on Remote client project). This made things worse — the visible lag between bg loading and playing increased. Reverted to 2026.2.27.5. The residual fractional delay on Remote client is a known limitation: bg decode latency is below the 0.1s drift threshold so the monitor never corrects it. If revisiting: try lowering drift threshold to 0.05s rather than the rAF re-sync approach.

queue.title: Mobile responsiveness
Transition polish
FG pool swap: new video plays in reverse for a few frames on adjacent switch
queue.note: On an adjacent sector switch (pool hit), the incoming FG video briefly plays in reverse for ~2–4 frames before resuming forward playback. Likely cause: the pool element was preloading and playing forward at time T; restoreVideoStateFromIndex then seeks it to a saved currentTime S where S < T (user previously saw an earlier point in this video). The backwards seek causes the browser to render intermediate frames in reverse order before settling at S. Possible fixes: (1) only restore saved state if S > pool element's current position — skip the seek if going backwards; (2) show a poster frame during the pool element's seek instead of letting it render mid-seek frames; (3) don't save/restore playback position per index at all for the dial — always start from the beginning on each visit.

queue.title: Dial ACTIVE→ENGAGED: use circular bounding box for proximity/hit detection instead of square
queue.note: Currently the ACTIVE→ENGAGED state transition uses a square bounding box. Replace with a circular distance check (distance from cursor to dial centre ≤ dial radius) so the engaged state only activates when the cursor is truly inside the circle, not the corners of the bounding square.

queue.title: Safari: HIT nav logo SVG appears larger than READY and PLAY
queue.note: Safari honours height="162" HTML attribute on SVG elements as the rendered height, overriding the 2rem flex container. HIT has the narrowest viewBox (316px vs 602/494), so preserveAspectRatio scaling makes its glyphs appear largest. Fix: .nav_logo-wrapper-2.is-nav svg { height: var(--logo-small-height); width: auto; } in ready-hit-play.css. CSS property beats HTML presentation attribute. Chrome unaffected.
